free_text: Charge distributions in low-energy nuclear fission
and their relevance to fission dynamics
- Cf -data on figs.
No details given.
G.C.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Corrections in all Subents.
HFR
Neutron guide of the ILL high-flux reactor.
Thermal Maxwellian neutron spectrum from
high-flux reactor.
100 mg U(3)-O(8) enriched to 97.68%,
encapsulated in a polyethylene tube. It was
counted at a distance of 3 cm from the Ge(Li)-
detector.
Gamma counting of the fission products.
Four different methods are used to deduce the
fission yields,
1.) variation of irradiation, cooling and counting
times allows determination of the independent
yield of (A,Z) and the cumulative yield of
(A,Z-1) by linear least squares fitting of
equation 1 of the Nucl.Phys.,A361(1981)213.
2.) Direct estimation of the independent yield of
(A,Z) if the cumulative yield of (A,Z-1) could
be neglected.
3.) Difference between the cumulative yields of
(A,Z) and (A,Z-1).
4.) Determination of the independent yield of
(a,z) from the known value of the cumulative
yield of (A,Z) and an evaluated value of
the cumulative yield of (A,Z-1).
100 cm3, 2.1 keV resolution at 1.4 MeV,
at the end of a fast pneumatic system.
Tbl.1 from Nucl.Phys.,A361(1981)213

for the monitor reaction.
Accelerator SAMES for production of

Neutron guide of the ILL high-flux reactor.
Deuteron-deuterium reaction for the
production of the 3 MeV neutrons. The purity of
the neutron beam was checked with activation
detectors. Contamination by low energy neutrons
was found to be negligible.

Four different methods are used to deduce the

1.) variation of irradiation, cooling and counting

yield of (A,Z) and an evaluated value of

SD: STATUS updated.
A 1.0E+06 fissions/min Cf-252 source, deposited
onto a thin Ni-backing.
Two categories of events were distinguished
The ones where a coincidence occurred between the
X-ray detector and the telescope, and the other
ones. From the coincidences the charge response
functions were deduced. These were used to unfold
the effective charge spectra.
Intrinsic germanium X-ray detector, allowed
to determine the charge of the fission fragments.
A dE-gas ionization chamber and
a surface barrier as E-detector.
K-alpha X-rays.
Fission fragments.
Occurence under the K-alpha X-ray peak of K-beta
lines of other elements and gamma-background.
Tbl.4 from Nucl.Phys.,A361(1981)213
SD: SF5=IND -> CHG in REACTION code.
STATUS updated.
High flux reactor.
Neutron guide of the high flux reactor.
Thermal Maxwellian neutron spectrum from
High flux reactor.
Th-229 target, 30 ug thick, deposited
over 1 cm**2 on a Be-backing.
Two categories of events were distinguished.
The ones were a coincidence occurred between the
X-ray detector and the telescope, and the other
ones. From the coincidences the charge response
functions were deduced. These were used to unfold
the effective charge spectra.
Intrinsic germanium X-ray detector. It allowed to
determine the charge of the fission fragment.
A dE-gas ionization chamber
and a surface barrier as E-detector.
K-alpha X-rays in coincidence with
fission fragments.
Occurence of K-beta lines from other elements and
gamma background under the K-alpha X-ray peak.
